title: From Drizzle to Disaster: A Deep Dive into Flood Insurance
body:
From Drizzle to Disaster: A Deep Dive into Flood Insurance

In this 3-hour webinar, flood insurance goes from confusing to crystal clear. Learn how flood risks are mapped, rated, and managed, from FEMA’s early efforts to the modern Risk Rating 2.0 model and private market alternatives. Examine policy forms, claims obstacles, and community resilience tools. Discover how to guide and protect clients with confidence, using real-world insights and practical strategies. Ethical considerations and tips for handling objections are built in, helping you tackle underserved markets and shifting risk realities head-on. Get ready for a detailed, engaging exploration of flood insurance from every angle.

Pricing

Webinar Details

From Drizzle to Disaster: A Deep Dive into Flood Insurance

In this 3-hour webinar, explore the evolution of flood insurance from its federal roots to today’s private market options. Unpack Risk Rating 2.0, map zones, policy forms, and key misconceptions. Gain clarity on rating, claims, and client advising while sharpening ethical decision-making skills in this fast-paced, informative session.

Topics

  • Explore the history, structure, and goals of the National Flood Insurance Program (NFIP)
  • Examine common misconceptions and communication challenges around flood risk
  • Analyze the impact of Risk Rating 2.0 on policy premiums and quoting practices
  • Discover private market flood options and how they differ from NFIP policies
  • Apply strategies for educating clients, addressing objections, and enhancing community resilience

Choose Your Date and Time

Wed, April 22 – 1:00 PM EST
